inline_comments.message: I'm thinking this should default to https://aai.api.simpledemo.openecomp.org:8443/aai
I'm thinking this should default to https://c1.vm1.sdnc.simpledemo.openecomp.org:8543/restconf/
This should be a sane default.
ditto and all of the following urls, passwords, usernames. They should all be sane default that are usable in default deploy.
Instead of hardcoding the name of a container, should we consider a template-based naming?
Instead of hardcoding the name of a container, should we consider a template-based naming?
Container should also have command and args as child elements to spin up the osdfapp.py application. The command is -- python osdfapp.py <args>

Please include the following new parameters in the config element.

# Credentials for the OOF placement service – SO. Newly added.
osdfPlacementSOUsername: so_test
osdfPlacementSOPassword: so_testpwd

# Credentials for the OOF CM scheduling service – Generic. Newly added
osdfCMSchedulerUsername: test1
osdfCMSchedulerPassword: testpwd1

We can use the following credentials for Conductor.
conductorUrl: https://oof-has-api.{{ include "common.namespace"}}:8091/v1/plans/
conductorUsername: admin1
conductorPassword: plan.15
conductorPingWaitTime: 60
conductorMaxRetries: 30

We can use the following credentials for the policy platform.
policyPlatformUrl: http://onap-pdp:8081/pdp/api/getConfig # Policy Dev platform URL
policyPlatformEnv: TEST  # Environment for policy platform
policyPlatformUsername: testpdp   # Policy platform username.
policyPlatformPassword: alpha123   # Policy platform password.
policyClientUsername: python   # For use with ClientAuth
policyClientPassword: test   # For use with ClientAuth

Messagereader credentials are the placeholder to support future applications. Roger from OOM suggested to capture NA as a value.
messageReaderHosts: NA
messageReaderTopic: NA
messageReaderAafUserId: NA
messageReaderAafPassword: NA

SDC credentials are also the placeholder to support future applications.
sdcUrl: NA
sdcUsername: NA
sdcPassword: NA
sdcONAPInstanceID: NA
Remove osdfPlacementUrl. However, we need to keep the osdfPlacementUsername and osdfPlacementPassword parameters.
To support the platform maturity requirements, we need at least two replicas.

Is it possible to refer the name of a container from its respective helm charts rather than hard coding them? If we hard-code the dependent container names, the deployment will fail if the name changes over time.
Anael: How would OSDF application start without command and args elements as a part of the containers section?

Should osdf dependencies vary for each deployment ?
What's the meaning of the TODO ?
I doubt <args> should be set as-is. What are the needed args ?
I don't think adding unused variables is a good idea. It adds unneeded noise.
These dependencies aren't going to be changed across deployments, it's not needed.
Setting the command and its args in the Dockerfile that generates the image is also an option. What are the that should be used ?
I am not sure who made that TODO comment and I don't understand what it means. OSDF dependencies should not vary. OSDF depends on the policy and oof-has-api containers for R2.

The application starts with the following command. To start the script, we need to run the following shell script.

#!/bin/bash
cd $(dirname $0)

# bash ../etc/make-certs.sh  # create the https certificates if they are not present

LOGS=logs
mkdir -p $LOGS

export OSDF_CONFIG_FILE=${1:-/opt/app/osdf_config.yaml}  # this file may be passed by invoker
[ ! -e "$OSDF_CONFIG_FILE" ] && unset OSDF_CONFIG_FILE

python osdfapp.py 2>$LOGS/err.log 1>$LOGS/out.log < /dev/null  # running the app
The dependencies remain the same across deployment.

Yes, the OSDF codebase contains a Dockerfile (https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=optf/osdf.git;a=blob;f=docker/Dockerfile;h=7a38ad8d5c4dbbfcf5d45d762caa0a3f87b5f642;hb=HEAD) that can also spin up the application.
Please make it clear this review is for OOF.
oof and oof-has have same chart description. What does HAS stand for?
space here and everywhere in this file.
should be portName (add a parameter in values.yaml)

service is defined twice. please combine.
let service name start with 'oof-', so it will be clear to which component it relates to.
Please make it ONAP OOF Music ...
shouldn't this be replaced with service name?
I do not see service.yaml for this chart.
Please make it start with oof-
Are you sure port 80 is free?
Can you please guide what is missing here?
Anael: Can you please address this comment?
Could you please add "OOF" so it would be clear what component this review is for?

Please remove all extra spaces.
Go over the review in Gerrit and eliminate all "red"-s
This still does not start with ONAP. Please make sure this is fixed in all charts.
Is ingress really enabled? Is there ingress.yaml file?
should be name and not fullname here.
